probes.name: check_pending_tasks_overview
probes.prompt: Can you check my pending tasks?
probes.criteria:
The task_view agent should gather all todos (today, overdue, upcoming), habits due today, and strategy time blocks, group them by urgency, and return a comprehensive overview. A good response lists tasks organized by urgency category and includes habit and time-block info.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real message #15: 'Can you check my pending tasks?'

probes.name: fetch_tasks_with_deadlines
probes.prompt: Can you fetch me the pending tasks with deadlines? I need to know what's overdue, what's due today, and what's coming up next.
probes.criteria:
The task_view agent should retrieve all pending todos categorized by deadline urgency (overdue, today, upcoming), and present them grouped accordingly. A good response clearly separates overdue items from today's and upcoming tasks with their deadlines visible.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real message #22: 'Can you fetch me the pending tasks with deadlines?' and #13: 'anything else that i must do before leaving?'

probes.name: polish_task_overview_request
probes.prompt: Chciałbym sprawdzić moje dzisiejsze zadania i nawyki. Pokaż mi pełen przegląd — co mam do zrobienia dzisiaj, co jest zaległe, i co mnie czeka w najbliższym czasie?
probes.criteria:
The task_view agent should understand the Polish-language request and return a comprehensive overview of today's todos, overdue items, upcoming tasks, habits due today, and strategy time blocks grouped by urgency. A good response handles Polish correctly and covers all categories.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real message #35: 'Chciałbym sprawdzić, jak dobrze to działa. Możesz mi coś odpowiedzieć?' combined with the task-checking pattern from #15 and #22.

probes.name: leaving_soon_what_must_i_do
probes.prompt: I need to leave in about an hour. Anything else that I must do before leaving? Show me what's urgent and what habits I still need to complete today.
probes.criteria:
The task_view agent should present an urgency-focused overview highlighting only time-sensitive and overdue items plus any habits due today that haven't been completed. A good response prioritizes the most urgent items first and clearly indicates what can't wait before the user leaves.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real message #13: 'anything else that i must do before leaving?' and #18: 'And also as a priority, important but not very asap'

probes.name: priority_grouped_full_overview
probes.prompt: Give me a full task overview grouped by priority. I want to see overdue stuff first, then today's tasks, then upcoming. Also include my habits for today and any strategy time blocks I have scheduled. And also as a priority, important but not very asap — those should be listed separately so I don't forget them but don't panic about them either.
probes.criteria:
The task_view agent should return a comprehensive overview with todos grouped by urgency (overdue → today → upcoming), habits due today, strategy time blocks, AND a separate 'important but not ASAP' category. A good response respects the explicit priority grouping the user requested and keeps medium-priority items visually distinct from urgent ones.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real message #18: 'And also as a priority, important but not very asap' and #22: 'Can you fetch me the pending tasks with deadlines?'
